title: Repetition and counter-reading
summary: A repeated term is interpreted alongside an exception and a source-context limitation.

concordance.excerpt: The speaker returns to the word home while questioning what it can mean.

interpretation.claim: The repetition may make belonging a contested idea.
interpretation.counterReading: One use is literal rather than symbolic.
interpretation.limitation: Frequency alone cannot establish the historical meaning of the term.

limitations: Word counts and concordance are descriptive evidence, not an interpretation or measure of importance.
Automated sentence boundaries may differ from editorial or poetic units.
transformations: English stopword filtering for ranked terms
full source text omitted from capture
